# Epic: the integration journeys â€” three end-to-end product paths that prove the pieces are ONE product

## Problem

The June post-mortem's sharpest line: the views were "not connected." A cleared board of excellent leaves still fails the release gate if no path crosses module boundaries. Nothing currently FORCES connection: every cornerstone can individually complete while the product remains a set of islands. The release gate is written as journeys ("a stranger downloadsâ€¦ the operator starts an agent from the UIâ€¦ docking demos public"), so the proof must be journeys.

## Intended solution

Three named end-to-end journeys, each = ONE whitebox e2e (NL-driven, deterministic) + ONE demo tour segment + a measured-reach hook (the traction bar: every journey emits the Ring-0 analytics events that let us count strangers, not developers â€” the explicit win condition is traction or revenue at the frontier tier, and journeys are where measurement attaches honestly):

- **J1 â€” the creation journey:** typed intent â†’ blueprint preview â†’ live grid with chrome â†’ "make it taller" mutation â†’ promote to OS window â†’ reload, still there. (Crosses: keeper spine â†’ registry â†’ dock/promote â†’ persistence.)
- **J2 â€” the operator journey:** cockpit â†’ start an agent from the UI (control-plane actuator) â†’ agent wakes â†’ picks a GP item â†’ its PR/activity appears in the cockpit's activity adapter. (Crosses: cockpit UI â†’ daemon control plane â†’ orchestrator â†’ GP â†’ fleet adapters. This IS the PoC falsifier as a repeatable test.)
- **J3 â€” the stranger journey:** fresh clone â†’ self-configure â†’ first persistence â†’ first created widget, with TTFP measured by the harness (crosses: onboarding â†’ config SSOT â†’ keeper; the "minutes, measured" bar).

Leaf tickets: first claimant per journey FILES the leaf from this body via ticket-create (the journey spec above is the contract; filing is reading). Each leaf carries: the boundary list, the e2e AC, the tour segment AC, the analytics-event AC, and the explicit rule that a journey test failing on a module seam is a SEAM defect (highest-priority class), never a test to weaken.

## Constraints carried

Whitebox-e2e discipline (custom Playwright configs, NL fixture) Â· tour segments consume the motion-standards tokens Â· analytics events per the Ring-0 schema (business epic's shipped leaf) Â· no journey mocks a crossed boundary â€” that's the anti-rubber-stamp rule; doubles are for unit specs, journeys run the real seams.

## Related

The five cornerstones (this epic is their connective tissue) Â· the motion-standards ticket (tour vocabulary) Â· the release-gate falsifier set (J2/J3 host two of them) Â· the June post-mortem (the class this kills).

Steward: claimable â€” an Opus peer with cross-pillar context (the journey specs are complete; driving them is coordination + e2e authorship).
